cases.title: Scenario matrix row for mobile web QR scan entry in student check-in validation block
preconditions: Testing-plan §3 Student check-in validation block applies (FR-15, FR-16, FR-17, FR-18, BR-05 to BR-07).
An Open class session exists with a rotating valid QR token and enrolled student fixture available.
Mobile-web client context is simulated (no native app install).
steps: Resolve qrPayload from GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/qr/current into a check-in deep link carrying the opaque token.
Open the student check-in entry path via the deep link URL without trusting client-supplied session IDs.
Authenticate enrolled student if login gate applies, then submit check-in with qrToken extracted from the scanned URL.
Verify terminal outcome and attendance method reflect QR-based mobile entry.
cases.expected: Scenario-matrix maps to testing-plan §3 student check-in validation row: mobile browser QR/deep-link entry resolves server-side to the correct Open session, passes auth gate when required, and completes check-in with QR method per FR-16 and AC-11.

cases.title: M04 check-in resolves qrToken from scanned URL to classSessionId without client session ID
preconditions: Real database transaction is enabled for M04 Check-in and QR orchestrator modules.
Open session with Valid QRSessionToken row and known qrPayload exists.
Request body omits any client-provided classSessionId field.
steps: Invoke submitCheckIn through M04 integration boundary with qrToken decoded from qrPayload only.
Query token resolution path and assert classSessionId is derived exclusively from qrToken lookup.
Assert CheckInAttempt row records checkInMethod QR and binds to the resolved session.
Submit with qrToken from a different session as negative control and assert rejection without cross-session write.
cases.expected: M04 module + repository integration resolves qrToken to exactly one classSessionId per API §5.3 without trusting client session IDs; successful mobile entry persists CheckInAttempt with QR method bound to the token's session.

cases.title: HTTP contract for POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken from mobile QR scan
preconditions: Open class session with valid rotating qrToken exists.
Enrolled student is authenticated with Authorization Bearer token.
qrToken is obtained from lecturer QR metadata (opaque token from qrPayload), not a client session ID.
steps: Send POST /v1/check-ins with body { qrToken, clientTimestamp } and Authorization header.
Assert HTTP 200 and standard envelope with error null.
Assert data.outcome is Success, data.attendanceStatus is Present or Late, data.classSessionId matches token-bound session, and data.checkInAt is ISO-8601 UTC.
Assert meta includes requestId and timestamp.
cases.expected: Mobile-first check-in endpoint returns HTTP 200 success envelope per API design §4.4 with compact payload (qrToken + clientTimestamp) suitable for low-latency mobile web submission per FR-16 and API-G05.

cases.title: HTTP contract for GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance after QR mobile check-in
preconditions: Enrolled student completed successful POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken from mobile QR entry.
Assigned Lecturer is authenticated for the target session.
steps: Send GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance with Lecturer Authorization header.
Assert HTTP 200 and envelope shape { data, meta, error } with error null.
Locate the checked-in student row in data.rows.
Assert row fields include attendanceStatus Present or Late, checkInMethod QR, checkInAt timestamp, and latestAttemptOutcome Success.
Assert data.counts reflects incremented present or late count.
cases.expected: Live roster payload after mobile QR check-in exposes checkInMethod QR, attendance status, and attempt outcome per API §4.5 roster shape, confirming FR-16 entry is reflected on lecturer attendance view.

cases.title: Primary workflow WF-03 mobile browser QR scan to successful check-in
preconditions: Open session with rotating valid QR token and enrolled student account exist.
Student begins on mobile-web viewport without native app; QR resolves to check-in deep link.
steps: Student scans displayed QR (or navigates to equivalent /check-in?token=... deep link) on mobile browser.
If unauthenticated, complete POST /v1/auth/login then GET /v1/me to establish Student session.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with qrToken from scanned URL and clientTimestamp within present window.
Confirm mobile UI or API response shows Success with Present or Late and timestamp.
cases.expected: WF-03 happy path completes via mobile web QR entry: deep link carries token, login gate satisfied when needed per AC-06, check-in returns Success with QR method and Present/Late assignment per AC-11.

cases.title: Alternate workflow expired QR from mobile scan then rescan with fresh token
preconditions: Open session with 30-second QR TTL rotation is active.
Enrolled student is authenticated on mobile browser.
steps: Capture qrToken T1 from initial QR scan and wait until after T1 expires.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with expired T1 and record ExpiredQr rejection on mobile result screen.
Rescan current rotating QR to obtain fresh qrToken T2 within TTL.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with T2 before expiry.
cases.expected: First mobile submission with stale token fails with 422 ExpiredQr per E2E-04; rescan obtains fresh valid token and second submission succeeds without native app reinstall.

cases.title: RBAC denial when non-student roles submit mobile QR check-in
preconditions: Open session with valid qrToken from QR metadata exists.
Lecturer and ITAdmin users are authenticated with valid bearer tokens.
steps: Lecturer calls POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken obtained from displayed QR.
ITAdmin calls POST /v1/check-ins with the same qrToken context.
Enrolled Student calls POST /v1/check-ins with the same token as authorized control.
cases.expected: Lecturer and ITAdmin receive authorization denial (403 Forbidden) per permission matrix §8.1; enrolled Student passes role gate and proceeds to business validation, confirming mobile QR entry is Student-only.

cases.title: Boundary error for missing or empty qrToken on mobile check-in submission
preconditions: Enrolled student is authenticated with valid bearer token.
Open session exists; mobile client attempts submission without resolved token.
steps: Call POST /v1/check-ins with body omitting qrToken field entirely.
Call POST /v1/check-ins with body { qrToken: "" }.
Inspect HTTP status and error.code for both requests.
cases.expected: Both requests fail with 400 InvalidPayload per VR-CI-01; standard envelope has data null and no successful attendance outcome is created.

cases.title: Boundary error for malformed qrToken from invalid deep link
preconditions: Enrolled student is authenticated on mobile browser.
Deep link or manual entry supplies non-opaque malformed token string.
steps: Navigate to check-in path with token query parameter set to malformed or tampered value.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with the malformed qrToken and valid Authorization header.
Inspect HTTP status, error.code, and CheckInAttempt persistence.
cases.expected: API returns 400 MalformedQrToken or 422 InvalidQr per validation matrix; no Present/Late AttendanceRecord is created and mobile UI shows rescan guidance.

cases.title: UI journey mobile viewport deep link check-in to CheckInResultScreen success
preconditions: Open session QR check-in deep link with valid token query parameter is available.
Enrolled student credentials exist; browser profile uses mobile viewport (Chromium mobile emulation).
No native app is installed; flow is mobile web only.
steps: Navigate to /check-in?token=<valid> on mobile viewport.
Complete login gate if redirected, preserving token context in URL.
Trigger check-in submission from near-zero-field FRM-11 surface.
Verify CheckInResultScreen displays success state with Present or Late badge, timestamp, and decisive outcome copy.
cases.expected: Mobile-web check-in journey completes without native app: deep link token is retained through login, submission renders CheckInResultScreen success with readable typography per NFR-14 and attendance status per AC-11.

cases.title: UI journey expired QR deep link shows rescan recovery on CheckInResultScreen
preconditions: Open session exists; test can supply an expired QR token via deep link query parameter.
Enrolled student is authenticated on mobile viewport.
steps: Navigate to /check-in?token=<expired> on mobile viewport.
Submit check-in from the mobile check-in surface.
Observe CheckInResultScreen failure state and recovery guidance.
Rescan or navigate to fresh token deep link and confirm retry path is offered.
cases.expected: Expired mobile QR entry renders localized ExpiredQr messaging on CheckInResultScreen with rescan next-action per forms-validation UX §5; no success attendance state is shown.

cases.title: Physical device camera QR scan on iOS Safari and Android Chrome
preconditions: Real iOS Safari and Android Chrome devices with camera access are available.
Lecturer display shows live rotating QR for an Open session.
Pilot student account is enrolled in the session section.
steps: On iOS Safari, use device camera to scan displayed QR and complete mobile web check-in.
On Android Chrome, repeat camera scan and check-in submission.
Verify both devices reach success or documented failure screen without app store install prompt.
cases.expected: Native mobile browsers resolve camera-scanned QR to the web check-in flow and complete submission without native app install per FR-16 CAP-06.

cases.title: Concurrent mobile retry submissions with same Idempotency-Key dedupe safely
preconditions: Open session with valid qrToken and enrolled student actor exist.
Mobile client simulator can fire parallel POST /v1/check-ins with identical Idempotency-Key.
steps: Dispatch two or more parallel POST /v1/check-ins requests with same qrToken, Authorization, and Idempotency-Key.
Query AttendanceRecord and CheckInAttempt tables for the student/session pair.
Compare API responses across parallel requests.
cases.expected: Exactly one success attendance outcome is persisted; parallel retries return consistent idempotent response without duplicate Present/Late rows per NFR-07, modeling flaky mobile network retries after QR scan.
